title: Uniformity in h = g_{1/m} and the mollifier limit m -> infinity

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Where:** final paragraph "Taking now $h=g_{1/m}$ and passing to the limit as $m\to\infty$ ...".

The stated theorem concerns the skew equation with merely $\mathcal C^{-1}$ nonlinearity. The proof works with smooth mollified $h=g_{1/m}$ and asserts all constants depend only on $m$ (the $L_m$ exponent) and $\|h\|_{\mathcal C^{-1}}\le1$, not on the smoothness of $h$.

**Gap.** Two things are needed and only asserted: (a) **uniformity** — all of $\Gamma_0,\Gamma,C$ are independent of the mollification scale (every estimate genuinely uses only $\|h\|_{\mathcal C^{-1}}$); (b) **stability of the limit** — tightness/convergence of $u^{(m)}$ to a process solving the equation with rough drift, and persistence of the Asymptotic Strong Feller property (and of the unique-ergodicity conclusion) under $m\to\infty$. Provide the compactness/stability argument.
